dc:title: Random Candidate Selector
dc:description: This package implements a random candidate selector as a simple example for prioritization algorithm.

schema:creator.schema:name: Display Lab
schema:creator.schema:affiliation: Department of Learning Health Sciences, University of Michigan

koio:hasKnowledge.dc:title: Algorithm that randomly selectes a candidate from a set of candidates
koio:hasKnowledge.dc:description: This algorithm encodes the knowledge of randomized candidate selection from a structured RDF graph. It identifies all resources of type slowmo:Candidate that are considered valid by having at least one slowmo:AcceptableBy relationship, and then randomly selects one of these valid candidates. The selected candidate is annotated in the graph with a slowmo:Selected property set to True, effectively recording the choice within the RDF data. This knowledge captures both the criteria for candidate eligibility and the mechanism for non-deterministic selection, allowing systems to make controlled yet probabilistic decisions while preserving the provenance of the choice in the graph.

koio:hasTest.dc:title: Tests for the candidate selector algorithm
koio:hasTest.dc:description: Tests to confirm knowledge and services work

koio:hasDocumentation.dc:title: Test documentation
koio:hasDocumentation.dc:description: Describes the tests for the random candidate selector algorithm

koio:hasDocumentation.dc:title: RandomCandidateSelector Documentation
koio:hasDocumentation.dc:description: Describes how the `RandomCandidateSelector` is a utility class responsible for selecting a valid candidate from an RDF graph at random and marking it as selected.
